name: Bixie
description: Bixie, also known as Pi Yao or Pi Xiu, is a mythical hybrid creature considered a powerful protector in Chinese folklore. Often depicted as a winged lion with a dragon-like head, it is believed to possess the ability to attract wealth and ward off evil spirits. Bixie are seen as auspicious symbols of good fortune and are frequently displayed in homes and businesses to bring prosperity. These creatures are thought to have a voracious appetite only for gold, silver, and jewels, which they consume without excretion. This unique characteristic has led to their association with wealth accumulation and the prevention of financial loss. Bixie are often found guarding tombs and palaces, symbolizing their protective and powerful nature. They are considered loyal and obedient, serving their owners diligently by safeguarding their possessions and bringing them good luck. Bixie statues are commonly crafted from jade, bronze, or stone, and are carefully positioned to maximize their beneficial influence. The creature's fierce appearance is intended to intimidate negative forces, while its benevolent nature ensures the prosperity of those it protects. Different variations exist in appearance, sometimes featuring a single horn or two horns, and their gender is believed to influence their specific protective qualities.
subtitle: Auspicious creature of wealth and protection
appearance: Bixie typically resembles a winged lion with a dragon-like head. It has a strong, muscular body covered in scales or fur, and often features sharp teeth and claws. Its eyes are fierce and commanding, and it may have one or two horns on its head. The tail is usually thick and powerful, and the wings are large and feathered or leathery. Bixie statues are often adorned with intricate carvings and embellishments to enhance their auspicious appearance.
habitat: Bixie are not associated with a specific physical habitat in the natural world. Instead, they are primarily found in symbolic spaces such as homes, businesses, temples, and tombs. They are often placed near entrances or in areas where wealth is managed, to maximize their protective and wealth-attracting abilities.
behavior: Bixie are believed to be fiercely loyal and obedient to their owners. They are said to be constantly vigilant, protecting their owners from harm and attracting wealth and good fortune. They are believed to have a strong appetite for wealth and are always seeking opportunities to bring prosperity. They are also thought to be territorial and will fiercely defend their designated space from negative influences.

keyMyths.title: The Emperor's Bixie
keyMyths.description: Legend tells of an emperor whose Bixie helped him amass great wealth and protect his kingdom. The Bixie's loyalty and vigilance ensured the emperor's prosperity and security, making it a symbol of imperial power.

sources.author: L. Carrington Goodrich
sources.work: A Short History of the Chinese People
sources.description: Provides general context on Chinese cultural symbols and beliefs.
sources.text: Provides general context on Chinese cultural symbols and beliefs.
